verdict_rationale: Three blocking issues: (1) the `secret_service` attribute referenced in the app-wiring comment points to the wrong object — silent signature mismatch that unit tests cannot catch; (2) the plan adds `affected_nodes` to `ValidationCheck` but does NOT update `validation.py` to populate the field for `identity_node_advisory` checks — every provenance warning ships with empty `component_ids` and the jump-to-component feature is dead on arrival; (3) `mypy` and `ruff` are absent from the 14a commit steps. Five warnings and four recommendations follow.

blocking_issues.title: secret_service wiring comment points to wrong object; TypeError at runtime
blocking_issues.detail: The plan's app.py snippet says `secret_service=app.state.secret_service  # adapt to actual attr name`. Confirmed by inspection of app.py: `app.state.secret_service` is a raw `WebSecretService` with signature `list_refs(user_id, *, auth_provider_type: AuthProviderType)`. Every other consumer in the codebase (execution_service lines 232, 480; secrets/routes.py) passes `app.state.scoped_secret_resolver` — a `ScopedSecretResolver` with signature `list_refs(user_id)` (no `auth_provider_type`). The Protocol in Task 3 (`_SecretServiceLike.list_refs(user_id, *, auth_provider_type: str)`) matches only the raw service. If the implementer follows the scoped-resolver precedent, the Protocol check passes statically but runtime calls crash (`TypeError: list_refs() got unexpected keyword argument`). The MagicMock in `test_service.py` accepts any call signature and will not catch this. The integration test in Task 5 is the only safety net — and only if the fixture exercises a code path that calls `list_refs`. Fix: decide in the plan which object to pass and update the Protocol, the service constructor, and the wiring in a single commit. The preferred pattern (matching all other callers) is `scoped_secret_resolver` with the simpler two-argument `list_refs`. The Protocol's `auth_provider_type` kwarg then becomes redundant and should be dropped.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/secrets/service.py line 142: def list_refs(self, user_id: str, *, auth_provider_type: AuthProviderType)
src/elspeth/web/secrets/service.py line 312: def list_refs(self, user_id: str) — ScopedSecretResolver

blocking_issues.task: Task 3 (service.py) and implied ValidationCheck schema change
blocking_issues.title: affected_nodes field added to ValidationCheck but validation.py never populates it — jump-to-component is dead on arrival
blocking_issues.detail: The plan adds `affected_nodes: tuple[str, ...] = ()` to `ValidationCheck` in `execution/schemas.py`. The `_build_provenance_row` in service.py reads `c.affected_nodes` to build `component_ids` for the Provenance row. However, `validation.py` constructs `ValidationCheck(name=_CHECK_IDENTITY_NODE_ADVISORY, passed=True, detail=...)` at line 1244 WITHOUT passing `affected_nodes`. The default is `()`. Every provenance warning will therefore yield `component_ids=()` in the panel — the jump-to-component button in `ReadinessRowDetail` is permanently unreachable from provenance warnings. The plan acknowledges 'old check instances' at line 97 of 14a but frames this as a schema-version-skew scenario, not the shipping state. This is not a future risk; it is the guaranteed state at merge unless `validation.py` is updated in the same commit. Fix: in the commit that adds `affected_nodes` to `ValidationCheck`, also update `validation.py` around line 1248 to pass `affected_nodes=(identity_finding.node_id,)`. The `_find_identity_node_advisories()` result already provides `identity_finding.node_id`.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/execution/validation.py lines 1242-1253: ValidationCheck constructed without affected_nodes
src/elspeth/web/execution/schemas.py line 50-56: ValidationCheck has name, passed, detail only (no affected_nodes yet)
14a-phase-2a-backend.md line 97: acknowledges empty component_ids on 'old' instances but misframes it

blocking_issues.title: mypy and ruff absent from all 14a commit sequences
blocking_issues.priority_score: MEDIUM × MEDIUM × TRIVIAL = blocking by convention (CLAUDE.md requires quality gates)
blocking_issues.detail: The 14a plan lists `pytest` and `enforce_tier_model.py check` in Task 5, but neither `mypy src/` nor `ruff check src/elspeth/web/audit_readiness/` appear in any commit sequence for Tasks 1-4. The project's CLAUDE.md development section lists `mypy src/` and `ruff check src/` as standard quality gates. New Python packages must be typed and lint-clean before merge. Fix: add `mypy src/elspeth/web/audit_readiness/ src/elspeth/web/execution/schemas.py src/elspeth/web/config.py` and `ruff check src/elspeth/web/audit_readiness/` to the Step before commit in each task (or at a minimum, at the end of Task 5 after the integration tests pass).

warnings.title: Subset-of-catalog tests in tests/unit/ load the full plugin manifest — classification mismatch
warnings.detail: `test_external_boundary_transforms_subset_of_catalog` and `test_external_boundary_sinks_subset_of_catalog` call `PluginManager().register_builtin_plugins()` inside `tests/unit/`. This instantiates and registers every plugin in the codebase. Functionally it is an integration test (depends on plugin file structure, imports, and registration side effects) masquerading as a unit test. This violates the unit/integration test discipline documented in CLAUDE.md ('unit in tests/unit/, integration in tests/integration/'). The tests serve a critical build-gate function (allowlist drift detection) and should not be removed, but they belong in `tests/integration/web/` where the plugin infrastructure is already exercised. Low urgency if CI does not split by directory, but if integration tests are excluded from fast-feedback runs, this test will be skipped when it matters most.

warnings.title: In-flight fetch not cancelled when composition_version advances — last-write-wins race
warnings.detail: `loadSnapshot` uses a no-op guard: if the cached version matches the requested version, skip the fetch. But if a v1 fetch is in flight when the composition advances to v2 and `loadSnapshot` fires for v2, both fetches are concurrent. If the v1 response arrives after the v2 response, it overwrites `snapshotsBySession[sessionId]` with stale data (v1 snapshot keyed under the session, not the version). The `composition_version` field in the response is not checked against the requested version before writing to the store. Fix: after the `await fetchAuditReadiness(sessionId)` call, compare `snapshot.composition_version === compositionVersion` and discard the result if stale. Alternatively, use `AbortController` for cancellation. The test suite does not cover concurrent calls and would not catch this.

warnings.title: MagicMock unit tests for ReadinessService do not provide path-integrity coverage for the secret-service call chain
warnings.detail: The unit test uses `MagicMock` for all four dependencies. For the secret-service interaction specifically, `sec_svc.list_refs = MagicMock(return_value=list(inventory))` accepts any argument signature silently. This is where BLOCK-1's signature mismatch would hide. The integration tests in Task 5 are the only net, but they also depend on `client_with_session` fixtures that may not exercise the `list_refs` path if the session has no composition requiring secret resolution. Ensure the integration test includes a case where the composition references a secret, forcing a real `list_refs` call against the wired service.

warnings.title: test_is_deterministic cannot detect cross-process dict-order non-determinism
warnings.detail: `test_is_deterministic` runs `build_narrative` twice in the same process and asserts equality. This catches non-determinism from mutable defaults or randomness but not Python dict-ordering variance across interpreter restarts. However, the `build_narrative` implementation iterates over `state.nodes` (a `tuple`) and `state.outputs` (a `tuple`), both of which are ordered by construction. Frozenset/dict ordering is not used in the narrative builder. The risk is low and the test is adequate given the implementation. Flag as informational: if the builder is later extended to use `frozenset` or unordered collections, the determinism test must be augmented with cross-process verification.

warnings.title: compareDocumentPosition assertion is brittle to intermediate wrapper elements
warnings.detail: The test asserts `panel.compareDocumentPosition(tablist) === Node.DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING`. `compareDocumentPosition` is based on document tree order and is stable in the DOM. However, if the panel is wrapped in a React fragment or a conditional wrapper that sits *inside* the tablist's parent but the panel itself is a sibling of the wrapper (not of the tablist), the assertion could produce `DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING | DOCUMENT_POSITION_DISCONNECTED`. A more readable and stable assertion is to query the inspector container, collect all role-qualified descendants, and assert the panel's index precedes the tablist's index in the list. Alternatively, assert that the panel's DOM node appears in the `previousElementSibling` chain relative to the tablist. Low severity; the test will work in most JSdom layouts.

recommendations.title: Add integration test fixture for session-with-secret-ref to cover the secrets row code path
recommendations.detail: The existing integration test fixtures (`client_with_session`, `client_with_session_without_state`, `client_anonymous`) do not include a session whose composition has a secret reference. Without this fixture, the `secrets` row's `error`, `ok`, and `not_applicable` branches are only tested by the MagicMock unit tests. Add a `client_with_session_with_secret_ref` fixture that creates a composition referencing a secret name, and add an assertion that the secrets row transitions between `not_applicable` and `error` as the secret is registered and de-registered in the test environment's WebSecretService.

recommendations.title: Add test for all-not_applicable misleading-collapse edge case
recommendations.detail: A composition with only a source (no nodes, no outputs) will pass `hasCompositionContent`, trigger a fetch, and receive a snapshot where `plugin_trust` is `ok` (source=BOUNDARY→ok with detail) and several rows are `not_applicable`. The `anyActionable` flag will be false → panel collapses to 'Audit ready'. But this composition is not runnable (validation will fail with 'no sinks'). The plan notes this in the umbrella's risk table but the test suite does not exercise it. Add a case: source-only composition → snapshot with validation=error → verify panel does NOT collapse (anyActionable=true because validation=error). This confirms the error short-circuits the collapse.

recommendations.title: LookupError path in snapshot route is not observable for debugging
recommendations.detail: When `compute_snapshot` raises `LookupError`, the route catches it and raises `HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from None`. The `from None` suppresses the exception chain; no log, no telemetry, no audit entry is produced. For a production debugging scenario where a user reports 'the panel shows nothing', there is no server-side signal that the 404 was triggered. Per ELSPETH primacy, logging is last resort — but the route could at minimum emit a structlog debug line at the catch site. This is a recommendation, not a blocker, because the 404 is deterministic (session has no state) and the frontend displays the error state.

recommendations.title: parseResponse error mapping is not covered by AuditReadinessPanel tests
recommendations.detail: The `AuditReadinessPanel.test.tsx` mocks `api/auditReadiness` at the module level (`vi.mock('../../api/auditReadiness')`). This means `parseResponse`'s error-to-`ApiError` mapping code is never executed in the panel tests. The mapping IS tested in `auditReadiness.test.ts` (Task 2), so coverage is not zero. However, if the `ApiError.detail` field name changes in `types/api.ts`, the panel's `apiErr.detail ?? 'Failed to load…'` fallback may silently return the fallback string without any test failing. Cross-test: confirm `auditReadiness.test.ts` asserting `rejects.toMatchObject({status: 404})` also validates the `detail` field.

test_strategy_assessment.overall: TDD-shaped with genuine failing-test discipline for all tasks. RED → GREEN → commit structure is explicitly declared and appears followed.

gaps.gap: MagicMock tests do not catch signature mismatches on secret_service calls (see BLOCK-1)

Fixture names are acknowledged as placeholders to adapt; the IDOR test (cross-user session access) is the critical security case and must not be deferred. The plan says 'adapt fixture names below' — this is acceptable in a plan but the implementer must create the fixtures rather than skip the tests.
Plugin-manifest-loading tests are in tests/unit/ but behave as integration tests — see WARN-1
Concurrent-fetch race is not tested — see WARN-2
mypy and ruff missing from commit sequences — see BLOCK-3

positive_findings: test_is_deterministic for narrative builder is present
Exhaustive `never` arm on ReadinessRowId/ReadinessStatus discriminated unions provides build-time coverage for wire-schema drift
Subset-of-catalog test for plugin allowlist is a genuinely useful build-gate even if mislocated
Negative IDOR test (cross-user 404) is explicitly planned
Error-state rendering tests (alert role) present for both snapshot and explain flows

observability_assessment.telemetry_deferral: ACCEPTED — Phase 8 owns the telemetry pass. The audit-readiness panel is a read-only UI projection of checks ELSPETH already records; no new decision events are introduced that require audit-trail entries.

error_path_observability.logging: Stored in state.error, surfaced as role=alert to user
error_path_observability.status: ACCEPTABLE — frontend surfaces the error; structlog is not the right channel for UI-layer failures

idor_protection.detail: execution/routes.py line 90-104 confirms the existing _verify_session_ownership returns 404 in both the ValueError and the ownership-mismatch path. The plan correctly extracts this to sessions/ownership.py preserving the invariant.

xss.detail: Narrative content is rendered via React JSX interpolation (no dangerouslySetInnerHTML found in any plan code block). Row detail strings are rendered in <pre> elements via JSX. No XSS vector introduced.

user_id_scoping.status: CORRECT — route uses user.user_id from get_current_user dependency

plugin_name_injection.status: CLEAN — narrative builder writes plugin names from CompositionState (Tier 1) into strings that are rendered as text

localstorage_token.status: NOTED — not introduced by Phase 2; mirrors existing pattern per plan acknowledgement

empty_composition.test_coverage: Covered in AuditReadinessPanel.test.tsx 'renders nothing when the composition is empty'

source_only_composition_misleading_colla.plan_handling: Partially addressed (validation row will be error because no sinks → anyActionable=true → panel expands)
source_only_composition_misleading_colla.status: The misleading-collapse case is self-mitigating in practice because an unsinkable composition fails validation, but a test would confirm the invariant

all_not_applicable_snapshot.plan_handling: collapsed to Audit ready — retention and llm_interpretations are always not_applicable in Phase 2A; the 'misleading ready' scenario requires all six rows to be not_applicable, which cannot happen unless validation is also not_applicable (it is never not_applicable — only ok or error)
all_not_applicable_snapshot.status: Not a real risk given the six-row truth table

affected_nodes_empty_on_provenance_warni.plan_handling: Acknowledged but misframed as 'old instances' — see BLOCK-2

validate_button_side_effects_removal.plan_handling: Task 8 explicitly deletes handleValidate and callers, trusting Phase 3A subscriptions.ts. Cross-link documented.
validate_button_side_effects_removal.status: ACCEPTABLE — the dependency is explicit and the 'block-commit-if-not-migrated' policy prevents silent stranding

defensive_programming.location: auditReadinessStore.ts — apiErr.detail ?? 'Failed to load audit readiness.'
defensive_programming.assessment: ACCEPTABLE — this is at the Tier 3 API boundary (frontend consuming external HTTP response). The nullish coalescing is a trust-boundary coercion, not defensive programming in our own code.
ACCEPTABLE — compositionState can legitimately be null/undefined in the store when no session is active. The optional chaining guards against a real nullable state, not an 'it might be None' hedge.
ACCEPTABLE in test context — used to introspect plugin classes not guaranteed to have the attribute. Closed-list invariant test.

MINOR CONCERN — NodeSpec.plugin could be None legitimately (unselected node). This coerces None to the string 'unknown' in the narrative, which is semantically correct (describes an unselected plugin) but uses the 'or' fallback pattern rather than an explicit None check. Acceptable at the narrative-text boundary.

defensive_programming.location: _describe_source(plugin: str | None) called when state.source is not None but state.source.plugin may be None
defensive_programming.assessment: CLEAN — the None branch is handled explicitly in _describe_source

anti_patterns_assessment.layer_imports: Plan runs enforce_tier_model.py check in Task 5 Step 5. All new imports are L3→L3 peer or L3→L1/L0 — no violations expected.
anti_patterns_assessment.freeze_guards: Not applicable — no frozen @dataclass instances introduced. Pydantic models use model_config, not frozen=True dataclass.

production_readiness_summary.error_handling: PARTIAL — service raises LookupError cleanly; route catches it. No logging at catch site (see REC-3).
production_readiness_summary.logging: ABSENT for error paths, CONSISTENT with ELSPETH primacy order (audit/telemetry first). Phase 8 deferred.
production_readiness_summary.configuration: CLEAN — new field payload_store_retention_days has a documented default and description.
production_readiness_summary.graceful_degradation: PRESENT — panel shows role=alert error; null-return for no session or empty composition.
production_readiness_summary.rollback_plan: NOT ADDRESSED — plan has no rollback section. For a UI panel, rollback = revert the merge and redeploy. Acceptable for this scope.
